overview.short: Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple (Sai Baba Birth Temple) is a temple in Pathri city of Parbhani district of Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple (Sai Baba Birth Temple) is a temple in Pathri city of Parbhani district of Maharashtra, India. It has been claimed that Pathri is the real birthplace of Sai Baba, which has been subject to controversy.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple, Pathri?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple, Pathri is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple, Pathri?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Nanded Airport (91 km) and the nearest railway station is Aurangabad Railway Station (137 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple, Pathri?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple, Pathri at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple, Pathri?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple, Pathri start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Sai Janmasthan Temple, Pathri?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pathri, Manwath.
